$description: Sidecar trigger index for operator_decisions standing orders. Authored alongside (not inside) operator_decisions because that table's scope_clamp is preserved verbatim per architecture-policy::*::scope_clamp_preserved_verbatim. Each entry maps a decision_key to one or more match conditions. The PreToolUse hook reads this file, matches tool inputs, emits a FrictionEvent tagged with the decision_key, and injects the order's title + rationale via additionalContext.
$why: Tonight (2026-04-27) the operator filed 11 architecture_policy decisions covering patterns the agent kept repeating. Eleven memos with no surfacing layer is the same as zero memos. This file is the surfacing layer.
$replay_target: When tonight's session is replayed against this file + the preact-orient-friction hook, at least 8 of the 11 corrections must fire as friction events the FIRST time the wrong action is attempted, not the third or fifth.
$update_protocol: When operator files a new architecture_policy decision via praxis_operator_decisions, add a corresponding entry here with structurally matchable triggers. If the policy is purely semantic (no shell pattern, no file glob), leave triggers empty; the entry still exists for /orient surfacing.

triggers.title: Local stack: 1-2s polls, no 30s sleeps
Fix the flow, don't wrap broken flows in monitors
Don't touch CLAUDECODE handling in sandbox
Claude Code agent: MCP only, no bash praxis CLI
Auth via docker mounts, never agent shell env
docker restart preserves cached env; use compose recreate
Check catalog before manual edits — if no wizard exists, edit is canonical
triggers.why:
Many runtime/surfaces/migration edits have wizard equivalents in the catalog. Before hand-editing, check:

  • New CQRS operation? → praxis_register_operation (lands data_dictionary_objects + authority_object_registry + operation_catalog_registry rows atomically; import-resolves handler_ref + input_model_ref BEFORE writing; idempotent on operation_ref).
  • Add an optional field to an existing op's input model? → praxis_evolve_operation_field (plan-only wizard; returns a complete file-by-file edit checklist).
  • Provider/model onboarding? → praxis_provider_onboard.
  • Field metadata / data-dictionary reproject? → praxis_data_dictionary.
  • Soft-retire an op? → praxis_retire_operation.

Hand-editing migrations to add catalog rows (operation_catalog_registry / authority_object_registry / data_dictionary_objects) drifts from the wizard's atomic helper, skips the import-resolve guard, and skips receipts. Hand-editing handler files where the wizard would also write the migration leaves the two out of sync. BUT — if you've checked the catalog and no wizard covers the change you need (novel runtime logic, new business rule, novel migration), the edit IS canonical. The pause is to enforce the check, not to block all edits.

Keywords that should trigger this check: 'add column', 'register operation', 'new operation', 'add field', 'evolve operation', 'CQRS handler', 'INSERT INTO operation_catalog_registry', 'INSERT INTO authority_object_registry'.

triggers.title: CLI and MCP are siblings, neither wraps the other
Sandbox Python matches platform Python (3.14)
Resolver ≠ rate-limiter — distinct layers
stdout-only ≠ submission
DeepSeek scope: direct API research only; OpenRouter compile only; nothing else
Never route Gemini 2.5 (any variant) for real work
DeepSeek is API-only — never via OpenRouter or CLI transport
API keys live in macOS Keychain (account=praxis), never plaintext
task_type_profile.avoid_list takes positive labels only, not capability gaps
Workflow specs use verify_command + outcome_goal; deliverable proof is mandatory
Praxis worker uses MCP bridge to host, not bundled praxis CLI inside
Compose synthesis decomposes by work volume into ~20 packet seeds, not step count
OpenRouter is invoked only from the Praxis app user surface (compile button)
When operator names a provider, use that exact provider — not the wired default
No monkey-patches or shim layers in production — fix root causes
Every change must actively reduce complexity, not just fix the bug
Valuable not overwhelming — lead with signal, omit noise
Reproduce end-to-end before reframing a bug as UX confusion
Supersede only the exact decision the operator named
Give build/no-build/defer/retire ranking before bulk-filing roadmap items
Default to autonomous; review gates are wrong-direction by default
Agent is not Praxis — chat-directed 'stop X' is not a directive to mutate Praxis.db
Dispatch investigation through Praxis agents, not Explore/general-purpose subagents
Operator directs; Claude executes — never punt syntax to operator
Default to mcp__praxis__praxis_workflow for dispatch from Claude Code
No recruiter / candidate-matching examples in Praxis
Sandboxes mint freshly-issued, lifetime-scoped creds at spawn time
Debates assign frontier-tier models, one per provider
Moon is the entire Praxis UI — not a tab, not a builder surface
Moon visual language: black/white, hollow nodes, compact-idle expand-on-interact
Praxis Engine moves to rented VPS; July 19 2026 external-user deadline
